title: Epic: mvp-cli — the jjf Rust binary
body:
Goal: a single static binary at `target/release/jjf` that does what
`git-bug` does plus what we know we need on top. Covers create / read /
list / update / comment / close on bugs, with `--json` on every command
and no interactive prompts ever.

# Approach

The CLI surface, in rough order of expected use:

- `jjf new -t "<title>" -F -` — create a bug from stdin body.
- `jjf show <id>` — print a single bug, plain text or `--json`.
- `jjf ls [--status open] [--label X]` — list, filter.
- `jjf update <id> --status closed --title "..."` — mutate scalar fields.
- `jjf comment <id> -F -` — append a comment from stdin.
- `jjf close <id>` / `jjf open <id>` — convenience for status.
- `jjf label add <id> <label>` / `jjf label rm <id> <label>`.

All commands take `--json` and emit one structured object (or array).
No `--interactive`, no prompts, no editor invocations. Mutations resolve
through the storage layer (`mvp-storage` epic) and end up as `Jjf-Op:`
trailers in jj commit descriptions.

CLI parsing: `clap` derive, one verb per subcommand. Errors via
`thiserror`; exit codes follow common conventions (2 for arg errors,
1 for runtime failure). JSON contract pinned in `docs/cli-json.md`.

# Child tickets

- `cli-skeleton` — Cargo workspace, `clap` scaffolding (shipped).
- `cli-new` / `cli-show` / `cli-ls` / `cli-status` / `cli-comment` /
  `cli-label` / `cli-update` — all shipped.
- `cli-json-output` — `--json` audit and `docs/cli-json.md` (shipped).
- `cli-replace-shim` — drop `bin/jjf` the shell script. **Still open.**
  Unblocked as of the git-bug cutover (`176f3c6` in archived refs).

# Depends on

- `mvp-storage` (write/read of bugs as `Jjf-Op:`-trailered commits).

# Blocks

- `agent-ergonomics` (which adds `ready`, `remember`, `claim` on top).
- `multi-client` (which exposes the same operations over an HTTP/WS API).

# Status

Verb set complete and JSON contract pinned. Only `cli-replace-shim` remains:
flip `bin/jjf` from a `git-bug` shell shim into a thin wrapper that exec's
the Rust `jjf` binary (or drops the shim entirely). Epic re-filed in jjforge
2026-06-22 during the git-bug cutover; full status-comment history including
the migration design call (option B = start fresh) lives in archived git-bug —
use `git-bug bug show c4f7fcb` against `refs/bugs/*`, and `git-bug bug show
d12031c` for the cutover decision.
